medical_history.chief_complaint: Unresponsive with low fingerstick glucose level (28 mg/dL)
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Initially presented with altered mental status due to hypoglycemia at age 3. Confirmed primary adrenal insufficiency with isolated glucocorticoid deficiency. Experienced several hypoglycemic episodes, some leading to seizures around age 6 during viral illness. Managed with hydrocortisone and dextrose supplementation.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Primary adrenal insufficiency, alacrima, achalasia, mild learning difficulty, attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder, migraines.
medical_history.family_history: Unremarkable with no reported adrenal insufficiency or sudden death.

physical_examination.general_examination: Normal vitals, no hyperpigmentation, hypopigmentation, goiter, organomegaly, or clitoromegaly. Tanner stage 1.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Bilateral adrenal gland atrophy and hyperdense liver on abdominal computed tomography scan. Normal upper gastrointestinal tract studies initially, later developed severe dysmotility confirmed by esophagram and manometry study.
